// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-12225 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-22T04:25:31Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-12225", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-12225 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:3540207; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-22, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
attck_notes: Derived from CVE description: broken access control
risk_score_reasoning: {"formula": "v1.0.0-deterministic", "inputs": {"cvss_score": 0.0, "epss_pct": 35.6, "kev_confirmed": false, "verified_actor": false, "real_ioc_count": 2}, "components": {"base_cvss": 0.0, "epss_boost": 1.068, "kev_boost": 0.0, "actor_boost": 0.0, "ioc_boost": 0.4}, "computed_score": 1.468, "notes": ["CVSS absent \u2014 base score is 0; add NVD enrichment for accurate scoring"]}
analyst_verdict: WARNING — Grade C: standard vulnerability advisory. Verified CVE, limited enrichment. Suitable for awareness/advisory tiers.

title: CISA Adds LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Vulnerability to KEV List Following Active Exploitation
description:
CISA has added a critical LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-54420, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog following evidence of active exploitation in the wild. The flaw affects shared hosting environments and poses a significant risk to servers running CloudLinux with CageFS isolation. The vulnerability is classified as a UNIX symbolic link (symlink) […] 
 The post  CISA Adds LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Vulnerability to KEV List Following Active Exploitation  appear

title: Critical WordPress Plugin Vulnerability Exposes 1 Million Sites to File Deletion Attacks
description:
A critical security vulnerability in the widely used Avada (Fusion) Builder WordPress plugin has exposed over 1 million websites to arbitrary file-deletion attacks, potentially leading to full-site compromise and remote code execution. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-8713 with a CVSS score of 9.1, was discovered by security researcher “daroo” and reported through the Wordfence Bug […] 
 The post  Critical WordPress Plugin Vulnerability Exposes 1 Million Sites to File Deletion Attacks  appeared first on

title: U.S. CISA adds Splunk Enterprise flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog and urges agencies to fix it by Sunday
description: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds Splunk Enterprise flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added a Splunk Enterprise flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-20253 (CVSS score of 9.8), to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog.
